finding_results.notes: Sentence openings across the chapter, especially in the creek-bed sequence, now lead with setting, body state, and objects instead of repeated pronoun-action starts. A local validation pass after revision found 0 sentence starts with 'He' out of 223 total sentences, well below the packet threshold.

Replaced the telling narration at the water-delivery moment ('The old Derry would have been talking already, would have been narrating the whole thing back to him in his looping, circular way...') with an actual Derry line in his established register: run-on syntax, domestic deflection to Pell's fever, trailing conjunction cut off mid-clause. The line shows Derry's natural voice leaking through his enforced silence — he reflexively starts a Pell story while giving water-drinking advice, then catches himself and shuts down. His withdrawal is now visibly active (mouth closing over the rest of the sentence, standing and leaving) rather than generic muteness. The Pell mention also gains dramatic charge from the earlier narration establishing that Derry hasn't said her name since before Caul.

pass_summaries.summary: Fixed the post-Caul Derry-distance continuity break, cut the bowl re-inventory down to minimal identification, and rebuilt the chapter's sentence-opening rhythm so the consumption scene leads with sensory and embodied detail instead of repeated 'He' openers.

Single finding addressed. Gave Derry a pressured line in his own idiolect at the water-delivery scene (line 207), replacing narrator exposition about what his voice used to sound like. The line uses his established run-on syntax and Pell-deflection habit, then shows him actively shutting it down. Minimal collateral change — surrounding narration trimmed only where it duplicated what the new dialogue now demonstrates.
Chapter 11 now strips the repeated architecture/building tic out of the flagged prose beats while preserving the later System-architecture reveal seed and the chapter's existing state shift.
